content_en:
~Umar further said; "I wish I were a hair in Abu Bakr's breast." (Reported by Musaddid.)

~Umar was also reported to have said: "The smell of Abu Bakr was far more better than the smell of musk."

* Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, is reported to have said: "The best of people after the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, are Abu Bakr and ~ Umar. Never should I be loved by a man who hates Abu Bakr and * Umar.”

“Hadith with an unbroken chain, of narrators.

“Ali narrates: “Never did I compete with Abu Bakr in doing good deeds but found that he outdid me." (Reported by At-Tabaraani.)

Ar-Rubayyi> Ibn Anas, may Allah be pleased with him, said: "We read about the Companions of Prophets, yet we did not find that any of them had a Companion like Abu Bakr AsSideeq " (Reported by Ibn * Asaakir.)

Az-Zuhri_ said, "One of the virtues of Abu Bakr was that he never had doubt concerning Allah."

Az-Zubayr Ibn Bakkaar said: "I heard some scholars say, the orators of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, were Abu Bakr As-Sideeq and ~ Ali Ibn Abi Taalib, may Allah be pleased with them both."

Ar-Rubayyi Ibn Anas said, “Abu Bakr is like rain wherever it falls it brings good."

Abu Bakr is Called As-Sideeq:

The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, was uplifted to the Heavens, just after the demise of the Prophet's uncle, Abu Taalib, and his wife Khadeejah, may Allah be pleased with her. It was a time of sadness and disconsolation for the Prophet, and the situation for Islam appeared to be dark. It was there that he was assured of the destiny of Islam.

The morning after his ascension, the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, told the Quraysh that he had been carried from Makkah to Al-Aqsa Mosque in Jerusalem, and

from there he had ascended to the heavens. He narrated to them what he had seen.

When the non-believing Quraysh heard of the Prophet 's ascension to heaven, they regarded it as an absurdity, and began to ridicule the Prophet for his declaration. "This is unbelievable!" they exclaimed. “It takes a month for a caravan to go the Levant (Ash-Shaam) and a month to return, how could you do the return journey in one night?"

There were also some Muslims who wavered in believing the ascension of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, to the heavens. Some of them went to Abu Bakr and informed him of the Prophet ‘s journey to Jerusalem and ascension to the heavens. Some expressed doubt about the veracity of the ascension, but when this question was posed to Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, he silenced them with his unforgettable words: "If he (the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him) says so, then he is truthful. There is nothing to be surprised about. I believe him when he says that he received a revelation from Heaven in an hour of the day or night. And that is beyond what you cavail at. "

It was on that occasion that he earned the title As-Sideeq. May Allah be pleased with him and rest his soul in peace.

Second of the Two:

Having heard the news of the success of Muslims in preaching Islam in Madeenah, the unbelievers at Makkah grew more and more furious and began to inflict intolerable atrocities on Muslims. The savagery and torture of the Quraysh escalated and the Muslims could not endure the severe maltreatment. Makkah was no longer a safe place for

Muslims to dwell in. At that point, Allah Almighty gave the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, permission to emigrate to Yathrib (Madeenah). In the name of religion the Muslims were to leave their homes and hearths, and begin a new life in another city.

The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, allowed Muslims to emigrate for Madeenah in batches. Only the Prophet. Abu Bakr, “Ali and the helpless honorable souls, who had been detained in confinement or were unable to break free from slavery, remained there. When Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, sought permission to emigrate to, the Prophet said to him, “Don't be in hurry, for it may be that Allah will give you a companion." Abu Bakr took this to imply that he was to accompany the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, in the journey. He felt happy and honored at the prospect of being the Prophet's traveling companion to Yathrib. He bought two camels, and made other preparations for their travel.

In the meantime, the Quraysh of Makkah made a plot to murder the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, but Almighty Allah foiled their evil conspiracy. The Quran makes mention of this plot:

Ly in i ad pf i 9p uth oy SE

"Remember How the Unbelievers plotted against thee, to keep thee in bonds, or slay thee, or get thee (of thy home). They plot and plan and Allah too plans, but the best of planners is Allah." (Al-Anfaal: 30)

One hot afternoon, the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, went to the house of his companion, Abu Bakr, and

gave him the tidings that the time for their emigration had come. "Allah has given me permission to leave the city and emigrate," the Prophet said. "Together with me? " asked Abu Bakr. "Together with you, " replied the Prophet. The two camels were saddled, and they hired ~ Abdullaah Ibn Urayqit, who then had not yet embraced Islam, to lead them on the way.

The assassins kept vigil all night long, waiting to pounce on the Prophet the moment he left his house. Every now and then they peeped through a hole in the door to make sure that he was still lying on his bed. The Prophet and ~ Ali were soon aware of their presence; and the Prophet picked up a cloak that he used to sleep with and gave it to * Ali, saying, "Sleep on my bed, and wrap yourself in this green cloak of mine. Sleep in it, and no harm shall befall you." Then the Prophet stepped out of his house casting a handful of dust at the assassins and began to recite:

{6} ged bine le 0} aaa Gal a (1) Sout OTD Ve

"Ya-seen. The Quran, full of wisdom. Thou art indeed one of the Messenger, on a straight way. " (Yaseen: 1-4)

He came to the words:

"And We have put a_ bar in front of them and a bar behind them, and further, we have covered them up, so that they

cannot see." (Yaseen: 9) At that point the Prophet went out

of the house and passed between them unnoticed and went on

At dawn, the assassins barged into the Prophet 's house and to their utter surprise, found that the person lying in the Prophet 's bed was “Ali, and not Muhammad, peace and blessings be upon him. This created a stir in the whole city.

Muhammad, peace and blessings be upon him, and his Companion Abu Bakr proceeded to hide in a neglected cave a few miles south of Makkah on Mount Thawr. When they had ventured a little beyond the precincts of Makkah, the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, halted his camel, looked back, and said: "Of all Allah's earth, you are the dearest place unto me and the dearest unto Allah, and had not my people driven me out of you, I would not have left you. "

Abu Bakr first walked into the cave of Thawr. He plugged all the holes, cleaned it and then asked the Prophet to step in. They stayed in that cave for three nights. During that period, ~Abdullaah, son of Abu Bakr, would go to see them daily after dusk and apprise them of the situation in Makkah. “Aamir Ibn Fuhayrah used to steal away unobserved every evening with a few goats to cover up the tracks of * Abdullaah and furnish them with a plentiful supply of milk. Asmaa’, daughter of Abu Bakr, also came with a bag of provisions; but she had forgotten to bring a rope, so she took off her girdle and cut it into two, using one to tie the bag and keeping the other for herself. *

Abu Bakr was very afraid for the Prophet , peace and blessings be upon him. One day, the enemy reached the mouth

*For this reason she carned the title “Dhatu-Nitagayn ", or the woman of the two

of the cave and came close to finding the two faithful souls. At that point, Abu Bakr whispered to the Prophet : "What if they were to look through the crevice and detect us? We are unarmed and at the bottom of the cave." Thereupon, the Prophet reassured him saying,

"Have no fear, for Allah is with us." (At-Tawbah: 40) Then the Prophet said to him, "What do you think of two when Allah is their third?"

Quraysh, on the other hand, were quite baffled and exasperated. A price was set upon the Prophet 's head. They offered a hundred camels as a reward for whoever could seize Muhammad, peace and blessings be upon him, and bring him back. This had spurred many to try their luck. Among those who were on the lookout for the Prophet and his Companion to win the reward was Suraqah Ibn Maalik. He, as Ibn AlQayyim narrates, received information that a party of four had been spotted on a certain route. So he decided to pursue it secretly so that he alone would gain the reward. He mounted one of the best horses and went in pursuit of the Prophet and Abu Bakr. On the way, the horse stumbled and Suraaqah fell. He later resumed the chase but was once again curbed the same way, yet the lust for material gain made him pay no heed to it. Again he swung himself into the saddle and galloped on at a breaking speed till he came quite close to the Prophet. Abu Bakr's heart palpitated and he told the Prophet, "O Messenger of Allah, we are done for. " But the Prophet said, "Be not cast down, for Allah is certainly with us.”

The repeated stumbling and falling from Suraqah's horse made him realize that the Prophet was protected against him,

and that it was a constant warning from Allah because of his evil design against the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him. He immediately changed and the sworn enemy turned into an honest believer. Approaching the Prophet with a penitent heart, Suraaqah begged for forgiveness in complete humility. The Prophet forgave him and confirmed it with a token written by Abu Bakr on a_ piece of parchment. Then Suraaqah hurried back to Makkah and tried to foil the attempts of those who went out in pursuit of the Prophet and his Companion.
content_id:
Umar lebih lanjut berkata: "Aku berharap menjadi sehelai rambut di dada Abu Bakr." (Diriwayatkan oleh Musaddid.)

Umar juga diriwayatkan pernah berkata: "Aroma Abu Bakr jauh lebih harum daripada aroma minyak kesturi."

Ali, semoga Allah meridhainya, diriwayatkan pernah berkata: "Manusia terbaik setelah Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, adalah Abu Bakr dan Umar. Janganlah seseorang yang membenci Abu Bakr dan Umar mengaku mencintaiku."

"Hadith dengan sanad yang bersambung tanpa terputus.

Ali meriwayatkan: "Tidak pernah aku bersaing dengan Abu Bakr dalam melakukan kebaikan kecuali aku mendapati beliau selalu mengunggulikku." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Thabarani.)

Ar-Rubayyi' bin Anas, semoga Allah meridhainya, berkata: "Kami membaca tentang para sahabat para Nabi, namun kami tidak menemukan seorang pun dari mereka yang memiliki sahabat seperti Abu Bakr Ash-Shiddiq." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu 'Asakir.)

Az-Zuhri berkata: "Salah satu keutamaan Abu Bakr adalah beliau tidak pernah ragu terhadap Allah."

Az-Zubayr bin Bakkar berkata: "Aku mendengar beberapa ulama berkata, para orator Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, adalah Abu Bakr Ash-Shiddiq dan Ali bin Abi Thalib, semoga Allah meridhai keduanya."

Ar-Rubayyi' bin Anas berkata: "Abu Bakr bagaikan hujan, di mana pun ia turun, ia membawa kebaikan."

Abu Bakr Dijuluki Ash-Shiddiq:

Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, diangkat ke langit, tepat setelah wafatnya paman Nabi, Abu Thalib, dan istrinya Khadijah, semoga Allah meridhainya. Itu adalah masa kesedihan dan kedukaan bagi Nabi, dan keadaan Islam tampak gelap. Di sanalah beliau diyakinkan tentang takdir Islam.

Pagi hari setelah mi'rajnya, Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, memberitahu kaum Quraisy bahwa beliau telah dibawa dari Makkah ke Masjid Al-Aqsha di Yerusalem, dan

dari sana beliau naik ke langit. Beliau menceritakan kepada mereka apa yang telah beliau lihat.

Ketika kaum Quraisy yang kafir mendengar tentang mi'raj Nabi ke langit, mereka menganggapnya sesuatu yang mustahil, dan mulai mengolok-olok Nabi atas pernyataannya. "Ini tidak bisa dipercaya!" seru mereka. "Diperlukan waktu sebulan bagi sebuah kafilah untuk pergi ke Syam dan sebulan untuk kembali, bagaimana mungkin engkau melakukan perjalanan pulang-pergi dalam satu malam?"

Ada juga sebagian Muslim yang goyah dalam mempercayai mi'raj Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, ke langit. Sebagian dari mereka mendatangi Abu Bakr dan memberitahunya tentang perjalanan Nabi ke Yerusalem dan mi'rajnya ke langit. Sebagian mengungkapkan keraguan tentang kebenaran mi'raj tersebut, namun ketika pertanyaan ini diajukan kepada Abu Bakr, semoga Allah meridhainya, beliau membungkam mereka dengan kata-katanya yang tak terlupakan: "Jika beliau (Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) mengatakan demikian, maka beliau benar. Tidak ada yang perlu diherankan. Aku mempercayainya ketika beliau mengatakan bahwa beliau menerima wahyu dari langit dalam sekejap siang atau malam. Dan itu jauh melampaui apa yang kalian pertanyakan."

Pada kesempatan itulah beliau mendapat gelar Ash-Shiddiq. Semoga Allah meridhainya dan mengistirahatkan jiwanya dalam kedamaian.

Yang Kedua dari Dua Orang:

Setelah mendengar berita keberhasilan kaum Muslim dalam mendakwahkan Islam di Madinah, orang-orang kafir di Makkah semakin murka dan mulai menimpakan kekejaman yang tak tertahankan kepada kaum Muslim. Kebiadaban dan penyiksaan kaum Quraisy meningkat dan kaum Muslim tidak mampu menanggung perlakuan buruk yang parah itu. Makkah bukan lagi tempat yang aman bagi

kaum Muslim untuk tinggal. Pada saat itulah, Allah Yang Maha Kuasa memberi izin kepada Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, untuk berhijrah ke Yatsrib (Madinah). Demi agama, kaum Muslim harus meninggalkan rumah dan tempat tinggal mereka, dan memulai kehidupan baru di kota lain.

Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, mengizinkan kaum Muslim untuk berhijrah ke Madinah secara berkelompok. Hanya Nabi, Abu Bakr, Ali dan jiwa-jiwa mulia yang tak berdaya, yang telah ditahan dalam kurungan atau tidak mampu membebaskan diri dari perbudakan, yang masih tersisa di sana. Ketika Abu Bakr, semoga Allah meridhainya, meminta izin untuk berhijrah, Nabi berkata kepadanya: "Jangan terburu-buru, karena bisa jadi Allah akan memberikanmu seorang teman perjalanan." Abu Bakr memahami ini sebagai isyarat bahwa beliau akan menemani Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, dalam perjalanan itu. Beliau merasa bahagia dan terhormat dengan kemungkinan menjadi teman perjalanan Nabi ke Yatsrib. Beliau membeli dua ekor unta, dan mempersiapkan segala keperluan perjalanan mereka.

Sementara itu, kaum Quraisy Makkah membuat rencana untuk membunuh Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, namun Allah Yang Maha Kuasa menggagalkan konspirasi jahat mereka. Al-Quran menyebutkan tentang rencana ini:

Ly in i ad pf i 9p uth oy SE

"Dan (ingatlah) ketika orang-orang kafir memikirkan tipu daya terhadapmu untuk menangkap dan memenjarakanmu, atau membunuhmu, atau mengusirmu. Mereka membuat tipu daya dan Allah membalas tipu daya mereka, dan Allah adalah sebaik-baik pembalas tipu daya." (Al-Anfal: 30)

Pada suatu siang yang terik, Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, pergi ke rumah sahabatnya, Abu Bakr, dan

menyampaikan kabar gembira bahwa waktu hijrah mereka telah tiba. "Allah telah memberiku izin untuk meninggalkan kota dan berhijrah," kata Nabi. "Bersamaku?" tanya Abu Bakr. "Bersamamu," jawab Nabi. Kedua unta dipasangi pelana, dan mereka menyewa Abdullah bin Urayqit, yang saat itu belum memeluk Islam, untuk memandu mereka di perjalanan.

Para pembunuh berjaga sepanjang malam, menunggu untuk menerkam Nabi begitu beliau keluar dari rumahnya. Sesekali mereka mengintip melalui celah pintu untuk memastikan bahwa beliau masih berbaring di tempat tidurnya. Nabi dan Ali segera menyadari kehadiran mereka; dan Nabi mengambil jubah yang biasa beliau gunakan untuk tidur dan memberikannya kepada Ali, seraya berkata: "Tidurlah di tempat tidurku, dan berselimutlah dengan jubah hijauku ini. Tidurlah dengannya, dan tidak ada bahaya yang akan menimpamu." Kemudian Nabi melangkah keluar dari rumahnya, melemparkan segenggam debu ke arah para pembunuh dan mulai membaca:

{6} ged bine le 0} aaa Gal a (1) Sout OTD Ve

"Yaa Siin. Demi Al-Quran yang penuh hikmah. Sesungguhnya engkau (Muhammad) adalah salah seorang dari rasul-rasul, (yang berada) di atas jalan yang lurus." (Yasin: 1-4)

Beliau sampai pada ayat:

"Dan Kami adakan di hadapan mereka dinding penghalang dan di belakang mereka dinding penghalang, dan Kami tutup (mata) mereka sehingga mereka

tidak dapat melihat." (Yasin: 9) Pada saat itulah Nabi keluar

dari rumah dan melewati mereka tanpa terlihat lalu terus berjalan.

Saat fajar, para pembunuh menerobos masuk ke rumah Nabi dan betapa terkejutnya mereka, mendapati bahwa orang yang berbaring di tempat tidur Nabi adalah Ali, bukan Muhammad, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam. Hal ini menimbulkan kegemparan di seluruh kota.

Muhammad, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, dan sahabatnya Abu Bakr bergegas bersembunyi di sebuah gua terpencil beberapa mil di selatan Makkah di Gunung Tsaur. Ketika mereka telah melewati sedikit batas wilayah Makkah, Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, menghentikan untanya, menoleh ke belakang, dan berkata: "Dari seluruh bumi Allah, engkaulah tempat yang paling kucintai dan paling dicintai Allah, dan seandainya kaumku tidak mengusirku darimu, aku tidak akan meninggalkanmu."

Abu Bakr lebih dahulu masuk ke dalam gua Tsaur. Beliau menutup semua lubang, membersihkannya, lalu mempersilakan Nabi untuk masuk. Mereka tinggal di gua itu selama tiga malam. Selama periode itu, Abdullah, putra Abu Bakr, mendatangi mereka setiap hari setelah senja dan memberitahu mereka tentang keadaan di Makkah. 'Amir bin Fuhairah biasa menyelinap pergi tanpa terlihat setiap petang dengan beberapa ekor kambing untuk menutupi jejak Abdullah dan menyediakan persediaan susu yang melimpah bagi mereka. Asma', putri Abu Bakr, juga datang dengan membawa kantong bekal; tetapi ia lupa membawa tali, maka ia melepas ikat pinggangnya dan memotongnya menjadi dua, menggunakan yang satu untuk mengikat kantong dan menyimpan yang satunya untuk dirinya sendiri. *

Abu Bakr sangat khawatir akan keselamatan Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam. Suatu hari, musuh sampai di mulut

*Karena alasan inilah ia mendapat gelar "Dzatun-Nithaqain", atau wanita pemilik dua ikat pinggang.

gua dan hampir menemukan dua jiwa yang setia itu. Pada saat itu, Abu Bakr berbisik kepada Nabi: "Bagaimana jika mereka mengintip melalui celah dan menemukan kita? Kita tidak bersenjata dan berada di dasar gua." Maka Nabi menenangkannya dengan bersabda:

"Janganlah berduka cita, sesungguhnya Allah bersama kita." (At-Taubah: 40) Kemudian Nabi berkata kepadanya: "Apa pendapatmu tentang dua orang yang Allah adalah yang ketiga di antara keduanya?"

Kaum Quraisy, di sisi lain, sangat kebingungan dan gusar. Hadiah ditetapkan atas kepala Nabi ﷺ. Mereka menawarkan seratus ekor unta sebagai imbalan bagi siapa saja yang dapat menangkap Muhammad ﷺ dan membawanya kembali. Hal ini mendorong banyak orang untuk mencoba peruntungannya. Di antara mereka yang mengintai Nabi dan sahabatnya untuk meraih hadiah tersebut adalah Suraqah bin Maalik. Sebagaimana diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Al-Qayyim, ia menerima informasi bahwa sekelompok empat orang telah terlihat di suatu jalur tertentu. Maka ia memutuskan untuk mengejar mereka secara diam-diam agar hanya dia sendiri yang mendapatkan hadiahnya. Ia menunggangi salah satu kuda terbaiknya dan mengejar Nabi serta Abu Bakr. Di tengah perjalanan, kudanya tersandung dan Suraqah terjatuh. Kemudian ia melanjutkan pengejarannya tetapi sekali lagi dihalangi dengan cara yang sama, namun nafsu akan keuntungan materi membuatnya tidak menghiraukan hal itu. Sekali lagi ia melompat ke pelana dan memacu kudanya dengan kecepatan penuh hingga ia sangat dekat dengan Nabi. Jantung Abu Bakr berdebar kencang dan ia berkata kepada Nabi, "Wahai Rasulullah, kita sudah tamat." Namun Nabi bersabda, "Janganlah bersedih, sesungguhnya Allah bersama kita."

Tersandung dan terjatuh berulang kali dari kudanya membuat Suraqah menyadari bahwa Nabi dilindungi darinya,

dan bahwa itu adalah peringatan terus-menerus dari Allah karena niat jahatnya terhadap Nabi ﷺ. Ia segera berubah dan musuh yang bersumpah itu berubah menjadi seorang mukmin yang jujur. Mendekati Nabi dengan hati yang penuh penyesalan, Suraqah memohon ampunan dengan penuh kerendahan hati. Nabi memaafkannya dan mengukuhkannya dengan sebuah tanda tertulis oleh Abu Bakr di atas selembar kulit. Kemudian Suraqah bergegas kembali ke Makkah dan berusaha menggagalkan upaya mereka yang pergi mengejar Nabi dan sahabatnya.
